description: Paytime is a stability-locked module. All changes require explicit authorization and a full regression gate before deployment.

protectedFiles.reason: Core calculation engine — PAYE, UIF, SDL, net pay. Any change here can silently corrupt all live payslips.
All payroll API endpoints — payslip generation, payrun execution, snapshot creation. Regressing these breaks payroll processing for all clients.
Data access layer and KV bridge. Changing isLocalKey() or the KV routing logic risks putting payroll business data into browser storage.
Payroll auth layer. AUTH.getSession() must always return {company_id, role}. Token must stay in native localStorage.
API call layer for payroll module. Changing headers, auth token read path, or base URL breaks all payroll API calls.
Houses the safeLocalStorage KV bridge installer. Changing bridge logic risks routing payroll data to browser storage.
PAYE reconciliation logic. Incorrect recon totals cause compliance failures and SARS filing errors.
Execute Payroll UI — the only authorized path to creating a finalized payroll snapshot. Regressions here break finalization entirely.
Employee payslip view, input forms, locked period display. Must correctly read is_locked from DB snapshot, not browser storage.
Payrun listing and period navigation. Incorrect period state display causes user confusion over locked vs open periods.
Shared auth — login, select-company, sso-launch. JWT must always embed companyId. Payroll company context depends on this.
Shared company routes. Company context is foundational to multi-tenant payroll isolation.
Shared auth middleware. authenticateToken sets req.companyId. requireCompany blocks null-company requests. Both are essential for multi-tenant payroll safety.
RBAC config. PAYROLL.VIEW and PAYROLL.PROCESS must always include correct roles. Accidental role removal locks out legitimate users.
Auth entry point. Token storage, redirect logic, and session initialisation all start here.
Company context selection. Incorrect company selection breaks multi-tenant isolation for all downstream payroll operations.
Employee listing and management entry point. Employee data integrity is foundational to correct payslip generation.
Company tax settings (SDL registration, PAYE reference, UIF number). These settings feed directly into payroll calculations.
Attendance records feed into hours-based payroll calculations and short-time deductions.
PAYE reconciliation UI. Incorrect display or data binding here causes SARS compliance filing errors.
Payroll items configuration (earnings, deductions, IRP5 codes). Changes here directly affect all payslip calculations.
Payroll financial reports. Data binding errors produce incorrect financial output for clients.
Net-to-gross payroll calculator. Uses the same calculation engine as Execute Payroll.
User and permission management. Incorrect role changes can grant or revoke payroll processing access.
Super-admin control panel. Has cross-company access scope — regressions here risk cross-tenant data exposure.
Main payroll dashboard. Navigation hub — layout or routing regressions affect access to all payroll functions.
Historical payroll import tool. Incorrect imports can corrupt historical snapshot data.
Splash/redirect page. Should redirect to login — any change to redirect logic affects auth entry path.
Attendance logic including hours calculation and short-time rules that cascade into payroll deductions.
Payroll items helper used in calculation flow. IRP5 code mapping and item classification logic live here.
Frontend RBAC check layer. Must mirror backend permissions.js. Divergence causes UI to show controls that the API will reject.
Feature flag control. Incorrect flag evaluation can silently disable payroll features for specific clients.
Audit trail logging for payroll actions. Regressions break compliance audit records.
Bank payment export formats. Incorrect file output causes failed EFT runs.
Payroll export formats (CSV, IRP5, reports). Incorrect output causes downstream processing failures.
Bulk employee/payroll data import utilities. Incorrect parsing can corrupt employee or payroll records.
Sean AI helper integration. Must not write payroll business data to browser storage or expose client data cross-tenant.
Shared sidebar renderer for all payroll pages. Navigation, company name display, and auth-dependent links rendered here.
Auto-narrative text generation for payslips and reports. Visual only — no calculation logic.
PDF header/footer branding. Visual only — does not affect calculated figures.
Mobile UI utilities. Visual responsiveness only.
Client-side version/update check. No payroll logic involved.
Particle canvas animation — visual atmosphere only. No payroll logic, no data access.
Core Paytime dark theme. Changes here affect visual presentation of all payroll UI including finalization state indicators.
Mobile responsive layout. Visual only.
Cosmic background visual layer — atmosphere only. No payroll logic, pointer-events: none.

regressionTests.name: Basic payslip calculation
regressionTests.description: Verify PAYE, UIF, SDL deductions and net pay are calculated correctly for a standard employee with a basic salary.

regressionTests.name: Execute Payroll matches payslip view
regressionTests.description: Confirm the figures shown on Execute Payroll exactly match the individual payslip view for every employee in the payrun.

regressionTests.name: PAYE tax bracket correctness
regressionTests.description: Test PAYE calculation across multiple tax brackets to confirm correct marginal rate application and rebate deductions.

regressionTests.name: UIF cap enforcement
regressionTests.description: Confirm UIF is capped at the SARS-prescribed ceiling regardless of employee earnings above the cap.

regressionTests.name: SDL registered vs exempt
regressionTests.description: Test that SDL is applied for SDL-registered companies and correctly waived for SDL-exempt companies based on company settings.

regressionTests.name: Overtime inclusion in gross and tax
regressionTests.description: Confirm that overtime pay is correctly added to gross income and flows through PAYE calculation.

regressionTests.name: Short time reduction cascade
regressionTests.description: Test that a short time reduction correctly reduces gross income, and that the reduction cascades through PAYE, UIF, and net pay calculations.

regressionTests.name: Voluntary tax override
regressionTests.description: Confirm that a voluntary additional tax amount correctly adds to PAYE on the payslip without affecting gross or other deductions.

regressionTests.name: Finalized snapshot immutability
regressionTests.description: Verify that a finalized payroll period (is_locked=true in payroll_snapshots) cannot be recalculated, modified, or overwritten through any UI path.

regressionTests.name: Payslip and Execute Payroll figures match exactly
regressionTests.description: End-to-end verification that individual payslip figures and Execute Payroll batch totals are identical down to the cent.

regressionTests.name: Company switching preserves correct context
regressionTests.description: After switching companies, verify that all payroll data loads belong to the newly selected company — not the previous one.

regressionTests.name: Multi-tenant: no cross-company data
regressionTests.description: Confirm that users cannot access or modify payroll data belonging to companies they are not authorized for, even with direct API calls.

regressionTests.name: No payroll data in browser storage
regressionTests.description: After a full payroll workflow (load, calculate, finalize), confirm that no payroll business data (amounts, status, config) exists in localStorage or sessionStorage.

regressionTests.name: PAYE recon totals match snapshot data
regressionTests.description: Verify that the PAYE reconciliation report totals match the underlying payroll_snapshots data for the selected period.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Writing payroll calculations, status, or config to localStorage. Business data must go to the API.
Writing payroll data to sessionStorage. No exceptions.
The KV bridge is not a substitute for SQL. Payroll business data must not transit through the KV bridge.
Finalization state must always be read from payroll_snapshots via the API. Reading is_locked from any browser storage is forbidden.
Company IDs must never be hardcoded. All company context must come from req.companyId (backend) or AUTH.getSession().company_id (frontend).
Once a payroll period is locked, its figures are immutable. No code path may recalculate or overwrite a locked snapshot.

deploymentGates: Change Impact Note completed before coding
All required regression tests run and passed
No browser storage added for payroll data

onlyFinalizationPath.description: The ONLY path that may set payroll_snapshots.is_locked = true
onlyFinalizationPath.path: UI Execute Payroll → POST /api/payroll/payruns → PayrollCalculationService → INSERT payroll_snapshots (is_locked: true)
forbidden: Direct DB updates from application code
